source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Consider ACK Sandboxed-Container for running untrusted workloads

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Consider ACK Sandboxed-Container for running untrusted workloads
input_row.description: Use	ACK	Sandboxed-Container	to	restrict	untrusted	workloads	as	an	additional	layer	of protection	when	running	in	a	multi-tenant	environment.
input_row.rationale: Sandboxed-Container	provides	an	alternative	to	the	Docker	runtime.	It	allows	you	to	run applications	in	a	sandboxed	and	lightweight	virtual	machine	that	has	a	dedicated	kernel. This	enhances	resource	isolation	and	improves	security.	Compared	with	Docker, SandboxedContainer	has	the	following	benefits: • Strong	isolation	based	on	sandboxed	and	lightweight	virtual	machines. • Network	Attached	Storage	(NAS)	file	systems	and	Alibaba	Cloud	disks	can	be mounted	to	Sandboxed-Container.	This	provides	the	same	storage	performance	as storage	volumes	that	are	mounted	to	the	host. • Compatibility	with	runC	in	terms	of	application	management. • High	performance	that	corresponds	to	90%	performance	of	applications	based	on runC. • The	same	user	experience	as	runC	in	terms	of	logging,	monitoring,	and	storage. • Support	for	RuntimeClass. • Easy	to	use	with	limited	expertise	that	is	required	to	use	virtual	machines. • Higher	stability	than	that	provided	by	Kata	Containers.
input_row.audit: Remediation: For	create	cluster: 1. Log	on	to	the	ACK	console	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/. 2. In	the	left-side	navigation	pane,	click	Clusters. 3. On	the	Clusters	page,	click	Create	Kubernetes	Cluster	in	the	upper-right	corner	of the	page. 4. On	the	Select	Cluster	Template	page,	find	Standard	Managed	Cluster	and	click	Create in	the	Standard	Managed	Cluster	section. 275	|	P a g e 5. On	the	ACK	managed	edition	tab,	configure	the	cluster	and	set	the	parameters	as required	in	the	following	list: • Zone:	Select	a	zone	where	Elastics	Compute	Service	(ECS)	Bare	Metal	instances	are deployed.	Sandboxed	containers	support	only	ECS	Bare	Metal	instances. • Kubernetes	Version:	Select	1.18.8-aliyun.1	or	later. • Container	Runtime:	Select	Sandboxed-Container. • Worker	Instance:	Add	worker	nodes	by	creating	new	ECS	instances. • Billing	Method:	Select	the	subscription	billing	method. • Instance	Type:	Select	ECS	Bare	Metal	instances. • Mount	Data	Disk:	Mount	a	data	disk	of	at	least	200	GiB.	We	recommend	that	you mount	a	data	disk	of	at	least	1	TB. • Operating	system:	By	default,	the	AliyunLinux	operating	system	is	used.	You	cannot change	the	operating	system. Default	Value: By	default,	Sandboxed	container	is	disabled References: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/142151.htm 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/140541.htm 3. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/160288.htm CIS	Controls: Version	7 18.9	Separate	Production	and	Non-Production	Systems Maintain	separate	environments	for	production	and	nonproduction	systems.	Developers should	not	have	unmonitored	access	to	production	environments. 276	|	P a g e
input_row.references: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/142151.htm 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/140541.htm 3. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/160288.htm CIS	Controls: Version	7 18.9	Separate	Production	and	Non-Production	Systems Maintain	separate	environments	for	production	and	nonproduction	systems.	Developers should	not	have	unmonitored	access	to	production	environments. 276	|	P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control suggests considering the use of ACK Sandboxed-Container for running untrusted workloads to provide additional protection in a multi-tenant environment. This requires evaluating business use cases and security policies, which can be subjective and context-specific. There are no specific API calls or CLI commands to directly check if Sandboxed-Container is enabled for "untrusted workloads" in an existing environment, as it depends heavily on operational practices and organizational policies for using such containers.

**MANUAL STEPS:**  
1. **Access the ACK Console:**
   - Log into the ACK console at [https://cs.console.aliyun.com/](https://cs.console.aliyun.com/).
   - Navigate to the "Clusters" section using the left-side navigation pane.
   
2. **Evaluate Cluster Configuration:**
   - Select the Kubernetes cluster you want to evaluate.
   - Review the configuration setting for "Container Runtime" to check if "Sandboxed-Container" is selected.
   - Ensure this runtime is particularly applied for workloads deemed as "untrusted" within the organization’s security policy documentation.

3. **Confirm Cluster Compatibility and Set-up:**
   - Verify that the cluster operates in ECS Bare Metal instances, as this is a requirement for Sandboxed-Containers.
   - Ensure the Kubernetes version is 1.18.8-aliyun.1 or later, as required for Sandboxed-Container compatibility.

4. **Review Policy Documentation:**
   - Cross-check organizational policies to ensure that the use of Sandboxed-Containers aligns with the security requirements for running untrusted workloads.
   - Verify documentation that specifies workloads requiring Sandboxed-Container usage.

5. **Validate Implementation:**
   - Confirm actual deployment in workloads by reviewing deployment descriptors and associated runtime classes within the applications running on the cluster.
   - Ensure that management practices enforce and validate the de facto use of Sandboxed-Containers for the relevant workloads.

No automated checks through Alibaba Cloud APIs or commands can directly verify this control as it stands, due to its dependence on workload-specific policy and organizational risk tolerance decisions.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** PARTIALLY_AGREE

- The classification of this control as a Manual audit approach is mostly correct due to the subjective nature of evaluating business use cases and security policies. However, some aspects of the control can be partially automated using Alibaba Cloud APIs and CLI tools.
- While there isn't a direct API or CLI command to check if Sandboxed-Container is used specifically for "untrusted workloads," certain elements like checking the container runtime configuration and verifying cluster compatibility can be automated.
- Alibaba Cloud SDK and CLI can be used to query cluster configurations and runtime settings, which can help automate parts of this assessment.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 7

- The manual steps provided are generally accurate, but they could be enhanced by incorporating some automation to check configurations.
- The Aliyun CLI or Alibaba Cloud CLI can be used to retrieve cluster information, such as the container runtime, which is not mentioned in the original assessment.
- The program naming convention is not applicable here as no specific program or script was mentioned. However, if automation were to be implemented, a descriptive name like `alicloud_ack_sandboxed_container_check` would be appropriate.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Automation Opportunities:** 
  - Use the Alibaba Cloud CLI to retrieve cluster details and check the container runtime setting. For example, the command `aliyun cs DescribeClusterDetail --ClusterId <cluster-id>` can be used to fetch cluster details, including the runtime configuration.
  - Automate the verification of Kubernetes version and ECS instance type using CLI commands or scripts.
  
- **Documentation Review:** 
  - The review of policy documentation and workload descriptors is inherently manual but could be supported by a checklist or template to ensure consistency.

- **Program Name Suggestion:** 
  - If automation scripts are developed, consider naming them with a clear and descriptive format, such as `alicloud_ack_sandboxed_container_runtime_check`.

**CONFIDENCE:** MEDIUM

- The assessment is mostly accurate, but there is room for improvement by incorporating partial automation which can increase efficiency and reduce human error.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- **Modify:**
  - Integrate partial automation using Alibaba Cloud CLI to check for cluster configurations and runtime settings.
  - Provide guidance on using CLI commands to fetch relevant data, which can complement the manual review process.
  - Update the assessment to reflect these automation opportunities while maintaining the manual review for policy and workload-specific evaluations.
